medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Palpitations emerged only after landing a round-off back somersault in a floor exercise. The palpitations were highly reproducible and occurred only after performing a round-off back somersault. The palpitations lasted for a few minutes and stopped spontaneously with resting.
medical_history.social_history: Promising gymnast, no substance use mentioned

physical_examination.general_examination: Chest roentgenogram showed no cardiac enlargement or congestion. Electrocardiogram showed a sinus rhythm of 75 beats/min without any significant abnormalities including delta waves.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Transthoracic echocardiography (TTE) revealed a tiny atrial septal defect (ASD) of the secondary foramen type with a size of 4 × 2.9 mm and a left-to-right blood shunt with a 1.4 of Qp/Qs ratio. Electrophysiologic study (EPS) revealed the existence of a retrograde ventriculo (V) – atrial (A) conduction with decremental properties, and high RA pacing under isoproterenol administration induced tachycardia.
